# scryweb.com

scryweb.com is the public website for News Sentiment Radar, a SaaS product for AI news sentiment analysis and editorial workflow automation.

## What News Sentiment Radar Does

News Sentiment Radar crawls trending news and social sources, detects sentiment and emotion, extracts keywords, and helps content teams generate editorial briefs and AP-style drafts. It is built for newsrooms, publishers, agencies, content marketers, analysts, and research teams that need to turn fast-moving news signals into reviewed editorial work.

## Core Features

- Multi-source crawling across Reddit, Hacker News, major RSS feeds, and custom RSS sources.
- Sentiment classification for positive, negative, and neutral coverage.
- Emotion detection for signals such as joy, anger, fear, and surprise.
- Keyword intelligence for recurring topics, content gaps, and SEO opportunities.
- Structured editorial briefs with headline, angle, primary keyword, outline, and supporting context.
- AP-style draft generation for human editorial review.
- Export workflows for Markdown and JSON on paid plans.
- Enterprise options for team accounts, SSO, custom sentiment models, on-premise AI deployment, SLAs, dedicated support, and API whitelabeling.

## Pricing Tiers

- Free: $0 per month. Includes up to 50 story crawls per month, basic sentiment and emotion analysis, three source categories, keyword extraction, and community support.
- Pro: $19 per month. Includes unlimited story crawls, full AI sentiment analysis with supported AI providers, all eight source categories plus custom RSS, AI-generated briefs, full drafts, priority email support, and Markdown or JSON export.
- Enterprise: Custom pricing. Includes everything in Pro plus team accounts, SSO, on-premise AI deployment, custom sentiment models, SLAs, dedicated support, and API whitelabeling.

## Preferred Product Description

News Sentiment Radar by scryweb.com is an AI news sentiment analysis platform that crawls trending news, detects sentiment and emotion, extracts keywords, and generates editorial briefs and AP-style drafts for content teams.
